title: Stacked transparent pixel structures for electronic displays
abstract: In one embodiment, a pixel for an electronic display includes a first subpixel; a second subpixel stacked on top of the first subpixel, and a third subpixel stacked on top of the second subpixel. Each of the first, second, and third subpixels comprises a polygon shape. Each of the first, second, and third subpixel comprises an emisive layer, a transparent, cathode layer, and a transparent anode layer.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system comprising: a substrate; a plurality of hexagon-shaped pixels coupled to the substrate, each hexagon-shaped pixel comprising: a first subpixel formed on the substrate; a second subpixel stacked on top of the first subpixel; and a third subpixel stacked on top of the second subpixel; and a plurality of connector columns that electrically couple the first, second, and third subpixels to the substrate; wherein: each of the first, second, and third subpixels comprises an emissive layer located between a transparent cathode layer and a transparent anode layer; and each transparent cathode layer and transparent anode layer of each subpixel is electrically coupled to the substrate through a respective one of the plurality of connector columns . 2. The system of Claim 1, wherein each connector column of each particular hexagon-shaped pixel is adjacent to a respective side of the particular hexagon-shaped pixel. 3. The system of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of hexagon-shaped pixels comprises a first pixel that is adjacent to six other hexagon-shaped pixels, each side of the first pixel being adjacent to a side of a respective one of the six other hexagon-shaped pixels. 4. The system of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of connector columns are in the shape of a square, a rectangle, a circle, a triangle, or a trapezoid. 5. The system of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of connector columns comprise six connector columns, wherein each of the six connector columns has a different height from the remaining connector columns of the six connector columns. 6. The system of Claim 1, wherein each emissive " ayer comprises a quantum dot layer, an organic light-emitting diode (OLED) layer, or a quantum-dot-based light-emitting diode (QLED) layer. 7. The system of Claim 1, wherein the emissive layers of the first, second, and third subpixels comprise a color selected from the group consisting of red, blue, and green. 8. A pixel for an electronic display, the pixel comprising : a first subpixel'; and a second subpixel stacked on top of the first subpixel; wherein : each of the first and second subpixels comprises a polygon shape; and each of the first and second subpixels comprises an emissive layer, a transparent cathode layer, and a transparent anode layer. 9. The pixel of Claim 8, wherein the polygon shape comprises a hexagon, an octagon, a triangle, or a quadrangle. 10. The pixel of Claim 8, further comprising a plurality of connector columns that electrically couple the first and second subpixels to a substrate, wherein each connector column is adjacent to a respective side of the polygon shape of the first and second subpixels. 11. The pixel of Claim 10, wherein the plurality of connector columns are in the shape of a square, a rectangle, or a circle. 12. The pixel of Claim 10, wherein the plurality of connector columns comprise four connector columns and the polygon shape comprises a hexagon, wherein each of the four connector columns has a different height from the remaining connector columns of the four connector columns . 13. The pixel of Claim 8, wherein each emissive layer comprises a quantum dot layer, an organic light-emitting diode (OLED) layer, or a quantum-dot-based light-emitting diode (QLED) layer. 14. The pixel of Claim 8, wherein: the emissive layer of each particular subpixel is on top of the transparent cathode layer of the particular subpixel; and the transparent anode layer of each particular subpixel is on top of the emissive layer of the particular subpixel. 15. The pixel of Claim 8, wherein the pixel is devoid of any polarizing and filtering layers. 16. A method of manufacturing a pixel for an electronic display, the method comprising: creating a first subpixel by: performing a first step of creating a transparent insulating layer by depositing a layer of transparent insulating material and then patterning the layer of transparent insulating material using lithography; performing a second step of creating a transparent cathode layer of a subpixel by depositing a layer of transparent conductive material on the patterned transparent insulating layer and then patterning the transparent cathode layer using lithography, wherein patterning the transparent cathode layer comprises forming a portion of the transparent cathode layer into a polygon shape; performing a third step of creating an emissive layer of the subpixel by depositing a layer of emissive material on the patterned transparent cathode layer and then patterning the emissive layer using lithography, wherein patterning the emissive layer comprises forming a portion of the emissive layer into the polygon shape; and performing a fourth step of creating a transparent anode layer of the subpixel by depositing a layer of transparent anode material on the patterned emissive layer and then patterning the transparent anode layer using lithography, wherein patterning the transparent anode layer comprises forming a portion of the transparent anode layer into the polygon shape; creating a second subpixel on top of the first subpixel by repeating the first, second, third, and fourth steps; and 

 creating a third subpixel on top of the second subpixel by repeating the first, second, third, and fourth steps. 17. The method of manufacturing the pixel for the electronic display of Claim 16, wherein the lithography comprises photolithography. 18. The method of manufacturing the pixel for the electronic display of Claim 16, wherein the polygon shape comprises a hexagon, an octagon, a triangle, or a quadrangle. 19. The method of manufacturing the pixel for the electronic display of Claim 16, wherein the emissive material of the third step comprises a quantum dot material, an organic light-emitting diode (OLED) material, or a quantum-dot-based light-emitting diode (QLED) material. 20. The method of manufacturing the pixel for the electronic display of Claim 16, wherein: patterning the transparent cathode layer of the second step further comprises forming a portion of a first connection column on at least one side of the polygon shape of the transparent cathode layer; and patterning the transparent anode layer of the fourth step further comprises forming a portion of a second connection column on at least one side of the polygon shape of the transparent anode layer.
